fields.lane: distributed layer-sharding SERVE (CAMELID_GEMMA4_WORKER/CAMELID_GEMMA4_SPLIT), master shard 0..24 on the primary 16GB M4 host, worker layers 24..48 + head on the second 16GB M4 (Tims-Mac-mini-2), wire protocol v1 over an ssh-forwarded TCP port (macOS Local Network privacy blocks freshly built ad-hoc binaries from direct LAN dials; loopback is exempt)
fields.results: model load HTTP 200 with validated worker handshake (333.7s incl. 12.7GB receipt hash and cold master-shard load); /v1/chat/completions non-streaming 'Paris' (cold 166.1s first request - cold mmap faults; warm second request 9.45s wall for a 12-token answer); /v1/completions greedy ids [9079, 236761, 107, 100, 45518, 107, 101, 6372, 563, 4338, 236761, 9079] (leading ids match the committed 12B greedy envelope; raw completions do not strip thinking channels by design); SSE streaming 17 chunks with finish_reason stop and [DONE]
fields.caveats: serve-lane wall-clock only; the 6.2-6.75 tok/s pair cadence claim remains the CLI two-Mac bundle's; no llama.cpp comparator in this smoke (the 12B comparator frontiers are recorded in the two-Mac parity bundle); single-node 16GB remains memory-bound

claim_boundary: Exact-row text-token generation within the checked basic_v1 prompt pack and API smoke envelope only. No bounded-context promotion, no performance claim beyond the raw logs, no multimodal input (fails closed with a typed error), no neighboring-row or Gemma-family support, no shared-memory claim (distributed mode is layer sharding).
